coverage.evidence: imports startDomain/activate, dispatchOutbox, handleNotification, unsubscribe core; dispatchOutbox is same fn prod SQS handler calls; handleNotification is prod SNS path; uses real makeProdDeps tx runners which enforce workspace-scoping.

coverage.criterion: Real DB, SES/SNS mocked at boundary
coverage.evidence: adminPool on localhost:5433; only SES faked (CountingSes), SNS payloads hand-built; integration ran (95-250ms durations) not skipped.

startDomain PENDING keeps onboarding; activate SUCCESS flips active+verified; dispatchOutbox 'send', ses.sends 1 with workspace config set, messages_log n=1, usage emails_sent=1. Non-active -> 'refuse', ses.sends 0.

hard bounce via handleNotification -> suppression hard_bounce + global_hard_bounces + profiles bounced + email_events; subsequent send to recipient -> 'skip', ses.sends 0 via real buildIsSuppressedQuery.
same email in A+B; unsubscribe suppresses A only; isSuppressed(A) true, (B) false; B dispatchOutbox 'send' ses.sends 1.

60 sends + 4 hard bounces (6.7%>5%, MIN_SENT_FOR_RATE 50 met) via real core -> WS_REP suspended, WS_A active; suspended offender then 'refuse'. Rate from messages_log sent + email_events bounces, not rigged.

recommendations: Add CI assert-not-skipped guard (gate is DATABASE_URL presence only).
Test 6b seeds messages_log denominator via raw INSERT; fuller e2e could route the 60 sends through dispatchOutbox too.
Consider asserting email_events idempotency (replayed SNS=one row) within this composed file (covered in feedback unit/integration already).
